teams.purpose: Operate AWS accounts and microservices with a specialist team: account administration, billing/cost, IAM/security, DynamoDB, serverless integration, observability, and IaC release checks.

Run ecommerce scanner workflows through Hermes: source discovery, adapter research, scrape profiles, endpoint/browser scrapes, Supabase contracts, change intelligence, alerts, scheduled jobs, read views, and verifier reports. The canonical operator phrase "process website" means create a gated Kanban workflow, not a monolithic CLI run.

process_website.description: Map a new ecommerce website, build or update scraper profile artifacts, run a first dry-run scrape, verify Langfuse and Supabase receipts, then propose production promotion gates.

child_cards.purpose: normalize target URL, slug, client context, run id, and artifact directory
run deterministic HTTP mapper and identify platform/API evidence
build browser runbook and collect screenshots, recordings, HAR, trace, and DOM snapshots when HTTP mapping is insufficient
research observed anti-bot provider using public docs, safe search, prior ecom second-brain intel, and retry context from the failing worker
create or update scrape profile, fixture, and manifest from mapping evidence
run the first dry-run scrape through the ecom repo CLI/scripts

collect browser recordings, traces, HAR/network logs, screenshots, DOM snapshots, command logs, and readback receipts into a manifest; optionally upload to GCS and send one-line Telegram only with explicit approval
validate Mall Scanner or generic ecom schema contracts before any live persistence
verify runtime datafeed health, scrape_runs linkage, Mall Scanner API visibility, and schedule/cadence consistency
verify trace visibility and Supabase readback receipts when writes are approved
propose cron/launchd, alert channel, and live-write activation without enabling them by default
produce final operator report with receipts, risks, and next approval needed

runtime_policy.optional_runner_caveat: Claude Code TUI is option B for intentional overflow or Claude Code credit usage. Do not make it the default team runner.
runtime_policy.activation_caveat: Primary agent teams run through the Hermes multi-agent workflow. Use the Claude Code TUI delegate only when Greg explicitly chooses option B. If the ecom service user lacks Claude Code executable/auth access, the optional greg-owned delegate can target --cwd /Users/ecom/repos/ecom. Do not copy Claude credentials between users.
runtime_policy.operational_alert_exception: The ecom team lead may send Greg a one-line Telegram alert without a separate approval receipt only when a process_website stage is a true blocker requiring operator action or the same workflow stage has failed twice. Specialists should record routine progress, first failures, handled anti-bot evidence, and deferred readback rationale in Kanban/Langfuse/report artifacts without Telegram noise.

required_common_skills.rule: Every ecom specialist card must load Langfuse review/reporting context. Supabase skills are required only for cards that touch persistence, readback, schema contracts, read views, reporting, or production promotion. Supabase writes still require explicit approval and gbauto-supabase readback.

teams.purpose: Route coding, PRD, validation, and agent-team work through TAC-primed Hermes profiles. The team grounds work in Tactical Agentic Coding sources before build, uses role-specialized handoffs, and produces validation receipts before dispatch or completion.
